text: US government research into the health consequences of radiation exposure from nuclear weaponry involved egregious violations of the human rights of the Marshallese people. Evidence suggests that radioactive fallout from Pacific testing likely reached other Micronesian islands as well. To address this legacy, the US government must declassify all archived data regarding the history of these tests, their atmospheric fallout patterns, and the subsequent health impacts on populations across Micronesia. Furthermore, the global health community must advocate for nuclear-armed states to honor their treaty obligations and pursue the total elimination of nuclear weapons. Finally, as a matter of social justice and a commitment to the fundamental right to health, the federal government should expand Medicaid eligibility to include all citizens of the Compact of Free Association nations.
